blocks.content: Eli had gone to real effort. Blankets laid out, a fire going in the center, lanterns strung between two poles he must have hauled out himself. Meilin was there already, talking to Savannah. Marcus and DeShawn from high school showed up three minutes after Kit and Daisy.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Thank you. Meilin picked the spacing.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: He measured it. I said yes or no.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: It needed to take that long.
Savannah caught Daisy's eye across the field. Both of them smiled.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: He's happy. That's what different looks like on him.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Are we doing this tonight or are we roasting me.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Both. Grab a drink. Fireworks start at dark.
The field filled in slowly. The fire crackled. The lanterns swayed when the breeze came through. Above them, the sky was purple at the edges, fading fast toward navy.
